cluster_ids.name: On/Off
cluster_ids.summary: Provides a binary control (On/Off) for a device. Features include Lighting (LT), DeadFrontBehavior (DF), and OffOnly (OFFONLY).

classifications.summary: A base application cluster that operates on a single endpoint to provide on/off functionality.

revisions.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; CCB 1555
FeatureMap global attribute support with Level Control and Lighting feature
New data model format and notation
Addition of Dead Front behavior and associated FeatureMap entry
Addition of OffOnly feature and associated FeatureMap entry

features.name: Lighting
features.summary: Behavior for lighting applications. On LevelControl command causing OnOff=FALSE, OnTime=0. On LevelControl command causing OnOff=TRUE & OnTime==0, OffWaitTime=0.

features.name: DeadFrontBehavior
features.summary: When OnOff=FALSE, other clusters MAY fail commands with INVALID_IN_STATE. Attribute values MAY change to best-effort values, but reports and events continue.

features.name: OffOnly
features.summary: Device supports the Off command, but SHALL NOT support the On and Toggle commands. Useful for devices that can only be turned off remotely due to regulations.

values.name: AcceptOnlyWhenOn
values.summary: Indicates a command is only accepted when in On state.

values.name: Off
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to FALSE

values.name: On
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to TRUE

values.name: Toggle
values.summary: If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE, set the OnOff attribute to TRUE. If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to TRUE, set the OnOff attribute to FALSE (toggle).

values.name: DelayedOffSlowFade
values.summary: 50% dim down in 0.8 seconds then fade to off in 12 seconds

values.name: DyingLightFadeOff
values.summary: 20% dim up in 0.5s then fade to off in 1 second

attributes.name: OnOff
attributes.summary: Indicates whether the device is turned on (TRUE) or off (FALSE). If the Scenes cluster is present, this attribute is part of the Scene Table ExtensionFieldSetStruct.

attributes.name: GlobalSceneControl
attributes.summary: Prevents storing an all-off state or overwriting settings with a global scene. Set to TRUE on commands that turn OnOff to TRUE (e.g., On, RecallScene) and FALSE on OffWithEffect.

attributes.name: OnTime
attributes.summary: Specifies the duration (in 1/10ths of a second) the On state is maintained before automatically transitioning to Off when using the OnWithTimedOff command.

attributes.name: OffWaitTime
attributes.summary: Specifies a guarded duration (in 1/10ths of a second) in the Off state to prevent an OnWithTimedOff command from re-triggering the On state.

attributes.name: StartUpOnOff
attributes.summary: Defines the device's OnOff state upon power-up. If null, the previous OnOff value is restored. This behavior does not apply to OTA reboots.

commands.name: Off
commands.summary: Turns the device off. Sets OnOff attribute to FALSE. If supported, also sets OnTime attribute to 0.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
set OnOff := FALSE
if OnTime attribute is supported then
  set OnTime := 0
end if

commands.name: On
commands.summary: Turns the device on. Sets OnOff attribute to TRUE. Resets OffWaitTime if OnTime was 0.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
if OffOnly feature is supported then
  return UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND
else
  set OnOff := TRUE
  if OnTime and OffWaitTime attributes are supported then
    if OnTime == 0 then
      set OffWaitTime := 0
    end if
  end if
end if

commands.name: Toggle
commands.summary: Toggles the device state. If OnOff is FALSE, sets to TRUE. If OnOff is TRUE, sets to FALSE.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
if OffOnly feature is supported then
  return UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND
else
  if OnOff == FALSE then
    set OnOff := TRUE
  else
    set OnOff := FALSE
  end if

  if OnTime and OffWaitTime attributes are supported then
    if OnOff == TRUE then
      set OnTime := 0
    else if OnTime == 0 then // OnOff is now FALSE
      set OffWaitTime := 0
    end if
  end if
end if

commands.name: OffWithEffect
commands.summary: Turns the device off with a specified fading effect. Behavior depends on the GlobalSceneControl attribute.

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: Specifies the fading effect to use, e.g., 'DelayedAllOff' or 'DyingLight'.

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.summary: Specifies a variant of the chosen effect. If unsupported, server uses the default variant.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
if GlobalSceneControl == TRUE then
  call store_current_settings_in_global_scene()
  set GlobalSceneControl := FALSE
  set OnOff := FALSE
  if OnTime attribute is supported then
    set OnTime := 0
  end if
else
  set OnOff := FALSE
end if

commands.name: OnWithRecallGlobalScene
commands.summary: Turns the device on by recalling its global scene. Discarded if GlobalSceneControl is already TRUE.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
if GlobalSceneControl == TRUE then
  discard command
else
  call ScenesManagement.recall_global_scene()
  set GlobalSceneControl := TRUE
  if OnTime and OffWaitTime attributes are supported then
    if OnTime == 0 then
      set OffWaitTime := 0
    end if
  end if
end if

commands.name: OnWithTimedOff
commands.summary: Turns device on for a specific duration (OnTime) and prevents it from turning back on for another duration (OffWaitTime).
commands.timing: Updates OnTime/OffWaitTime attributes every 1/10th of a second when active.

fields.name: OnOffControl
fields.summary: Control flags. Bit 0 (AcceptOnlyWhenOn): if 1, command is processed only if device is already on.

fields.name: OnTime
fields.summary: Specifies the duration in 1/10s for which the device should remain on.

fields.name: OffWaitTime
fields.summary: Specifies the duration in 1/10s during which the device, after turning off, should not respond to OnWithTimedOff commands.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
if OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 1 and OnOff == FALSE then
  discard command
  return
end if

if current OffWaitTime > 0 and OnOff == FALSE then
  set OffWaitTime := min(current OffWaitTime, command.OffWaitTime)
else
  set OnTime := max(current OnTime, command.OnTime)
  set OffWaitTime := command.OffWaitTime
  set OnOff := TRUE
end if

// Server then starts/updates a 100ms periodic timer to handle countdowns.

State Description.behavior: OnTime attribute is decremented every 1/10th second, unless its value equals 0xFFFF.
State Description.pseudocode: if OnTime != 0xFFFF then every 100ms: OnTime := OnTime - 1

State Description.behavior: OffWaitTime attribute is decremented every 1/10th second, unless its value equals 0xFFFF.
State Description.pseudocode: if OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF then every 100ms: OffWaitTime := OffWaitTime - 1

references.name: FeatureMap
references.description: A bitmap attribute that defines the supported features of the cluster. Bits include LT (Lighting), DF (DeadFrontBehavior), and OFFONLY (OffOnly).

references.name: Level Control
references.description: A cluster whose commands can affect this cluster's OnOff, OnTime, and OffWaitTime attributes when the Lighting (LT) feature is supported.

references.name: INVALID_IN_STATE
references.description: A status code returned by other clusters on the same endpoint when this cluster's OnOff attribute is FALSE and the DeadFrontBehavior feature is supported.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attr for cluster version (not defined here)

references.name: Data Model Spec
references.description: Base types like map8, enum8, bool, uint16 are defined in the core Data Model specification.

references.name: Scenes Management server cluster
references.description: Mentioned as a cluster that may be implemented on the same endpoint.

references.name: ExtensionFieldSetStruct
references.description: A struct from the Scenes Management cluster's Scene Table where the OnOff attribute SHALL be added if Scenes is supported.

references.name: Interaction Model
references.description: Defines the general structure of command invocation, direction, and status responses (e.g., UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND).

references.name: Scenes Management Cluster
references.description: Referenced by OnWithRecallGlobalScene command to recall a global scene, which updates the device state.

references.name: EffectIdentifierEnum
references.description: Enumeration defining fading effects for the OffWithEffect command.

references.name: DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum
references.description: Enumeration defining variants for a specific fading effect.

references.name: DyingLightEffectVariantEnum
references.description: Enumeration defining variants for a specific fading effect.

references.name: OnOffControlBitmap
references.description: Bitmap defining control options for the OnWithTimedOff command, such as the AcceptOnlyWhenOn bit.
